transcript:
Agent: ShopEasy customer service, Chloe speaking. How can I help you?

Customer: Hi. I received a blender I ordered last week and the motor is dead on arrival. It does not turn on at all.

Agent: Oh no, I am sorry to hear that, Daniel. That is definitely not the experience we want you to have. Let me pull up your order. Can I get your order number?

Customer: It is SE-779023.

Agent: Got it. I can see the order — the ProBlend 500. Can I ask, did you try a different power outlet just to rule that out?

Customer: Yes, I tried two different outlets. Nothing.

Agent: Thank you for checking. This sounds like a faulty unit. I have two options for you: I can ship a replacement blender with overnight delivery at no charge, or I can process a full refund to your original payment method. Which would you prefer?

Customer: I would like the replacement. I bought it as a gift and the birthday is this weekend.

Agent: Perfect. I have already placed the replacement order with overnight shipping — it will arrive tomorrow by 8 PM. You will receive a tracking email within the hour. For the defective unit, a prepaid return label will also be in that email. You have 30 days to send it back at no cost to you.

Customer: That is really quick, thank you.

Agent: Of course. I am also adding a $10 store credit to your account for the inconvenience. Is there anything else I can help you with?

Customer: No, that covers everything. Very happy with how this was handled.

Agent: I am glad we could sort it out quickly. Have a wonderful birthday celebration this weekend!
